summary: This week's AI news highlights significant advancements in AI infrastructure, robotics, and healthcare. NVIDIA and AMD are investing heavily in AI systems, while OpenAI is expanding its ChatGPT Health service. Lawmakers are also preparing to introduce an AI Kill Switch Act, sparking debates on AI regulation.
insight: The increasing investment in AI infrastructure and the expansion of AI services in healthcare suggest a growing trend towards AI adoption in various industries, which may lead to increased scrutiny and regulation of AI development and deployment.
highlights: NVIDIA and AMD are investing in AI systems, with NVIDIA launching a new Medical Physics Simulation framework and AMD investing up to $5 billion in Anthropic.
OpenAI is expanding its ChatGPT Health service, allowing users to connect their medical records and health-tracking information to the chatbot.
Lawmakers are preparing to introduce an AI Kill Switch Act, which would require AI companies to shut down or throttle their systems on orders from the Department of Homeland Security.

articles.description: AMD is challenging its chipmaker rival with a new rack-scale system that will start shipping to customers later this year.

articles.title: Google just had its first negative cash flow quarter due to massive AI spending
articles.description: Google continues to report big quarterly revenue, but its AI spending has skyrocketed.
